body.title: Consequence check: articles synthesis — 14 insights landing in practice
body.result: PARTIAL — HTML output artifacts working as designed; ~8 of 14 spawned tickets remain open (pe_chain eval split, habit quality gating etc.) — those are tracked in their own tickets, not a consequence; no confusion from HTML artifacts
body.description:
**What changed:** 14 tickets filed from synthesis of 20 external articles, spanning HTML output, audit finding codes, palace decay, trace lesson extraction, pe_chain evaluation split, habit quality gating, memory search RRF, and credential audit.

**Predicted unintended effects:** HTML planning artifacts may create browser-dependency friction for Akien reading specs in terminal; audit finding codes may add verbosity that obscures prose findings; RRF in memory_search may degrade known-keyword lookup performance if semantic signal is noisy; pe_chain evaluator split adds latency to every sprint close.

**Signals to watch:** Are HTML specs actually being generated and read, or are they being generated and ignored? Are audit finding codes showing up in consequence-check ticket descriptions? Is memory_search RRF measurably better in practice? Are LegacyDirectClaimErrors gone from channel?

**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-04. Look at: channel for LegacyDirectClaimErrors (should be zero), day-close-audit findings for new palace-decay step output, any pe_chain runs that triggered the evaluator loop.

**Affected files:** None — observation only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe predicted effects; annotate outcome as occurred / clear / partial and close
**Test plan:** No tests — observation ticket
